home.title: Young Guardians of Our Waters
home.subtitle: Youth-led aquatic conservation dedicated to marine education, coastal restoration, and community engagement in Southern California.
home.heroImageAlt: Golden sunlight on Southern California coastline with gentle waves
home.missionProtectDescription: Leading hands-on conservation projects along the Southern California coastline — from beach cleanups to habitat restoration.

home.missionEducateDescription: Creating engaging learning resources about marine species, ecosystems, and the science behind conservation efforts.
home.missionEducateImageAlt: Young people exploring nature and learning about coastal ecosystems
home.missionConnectDescription: Building a community of young conservationists, families, and partners committed to protecting our waterways.
home.missionConnectImageAlt: Group of people gathered together at a sunny California beach

about.title: About Orca Child in the Wild
about.description: Learn about our mission, team, and the story behind our youth-led conservation efforts.
about.originText: Orca Child in the Wild was founded by Jordyn Rosario with a simple but powerful vision: young people can lead the way in protecting our waterways. What started as a family passion for ocean conservation has grown into a youth-run nonprofit dedicated to education, restoration, and community engagement across Southern California.
about.milestone1: Organization founded by Jordyn Rosario and family
about.milestone2: First beach cleanup event along the Southern California coast
about.milestone3: Website launched to connect youth conservationists
about.missionPreviewText: To inspire and empower young people to protect and restore aquatic ecosystems through hands-on conservation, science-based education, and community collaboration.
about.visionText: A Southern California coastline where thriving marine ecosystems and engaged communities coexist — led by the next generation of environmental stewards.
about.valueYouthLedDesc: Young people are not just the future — they are capable leaders today. We center youth voices in every decision.
about.valueScienceBasedDesc: Our conservation efforts are grounded in scientific research and data, ensuring our work creates real, measurable impact.
about.valueCommunityDesc: Lasting change happens when communities come together. We build partnerships with families, schools, and organizations.
about.valueInclusionDesc: Conservation belongs to everyone. We ensure our programs are accessible, welcoming, and representative of our diverse region.
about.valueTransparencyDesc: As a nonprofit, we are accountable to our community. We share our progress, finances, and decision-making openly.
about.focusAreaDescription: We operate along the Southern California coastline, from Los Angeles to San Diego — one of the most biodiverse marine regions in North America.
about.focusAreaMapAlt: Map showing OCINW focus area from Los Angeles to San Diego
about.teamDescription: Meet the passionate young people and mentors behind Orca Child in the Wild.
about.teamJoinDescription: Interested in joining our mission? We are always looking for passionate volunteers and advisors.
about.missionDescription: Our mission, vision, core values, and strategic goals guide everything we do.
about.goal1Desc: Grow our volunteer base to 500+ active youth conservationists across Southern California by engaging schools, community groups, and families.
about.goal2Desc: Lead monthly conservation projects targeting the most vulnerable coastal and freshwater ecosystems in our region.
about.goal3Desc: Develop a comprehensive, bilingual learning library covering marine species, ecosystems, and conservation science.
about.goal4Desc: Partner with 20+ local organizations, schools, and agencies to amplify our impact and share resources.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: Have a question, idea, or want to partner with us? We would love to hear from you.
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what's on your mind...
contact.successMessage: Thank you for reaching out. We will get back to you as soon as possible.
contact.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
contact.validationNameMin: Name must be at least 2 characters.
contact.validationEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
contact.validationSubjectMin: Subject must be at least 5 characters.
contact.validationMessageMin: Message must be at least 10 characters.
contact.faq1Answer: Youth ages 8-18 can participate in our programs. Volunteers under 13 need parental consent, and all minors should have a parent or guardian aware of their participation.
contact.faq2Question: Do I need experience to volunteer?
contact.faq2Answer: No experience needed! We provide all training and equipment for our conservation activities. Just bring enthusiasm and a willingness to learn.
contact.faq3Question: Where do your events take place?
contact.faq3Answer: Our events take place along the Southern California coast, from Los Angeles to San Diego. Specific locations are announced for each event.
contact.faq4Answer: 100% of donations go directly to conservation projects, educational programs, and community events. We are committed to full transparency — check our annual reports for details.
contact.faq5Question: Can schools or groups partner with OCINW?
contact.faq5Answer: Absolutely! We love partnering with schools, scout troops, community groups, and other organizations. Reach out using the form above or email us directly.

weather.pageDescription: Real-time weather conditions and tide predictions for Southern California beaches.
weather.locationExplain: We use your location to show weather and tides for your nearest beach.
weather.locationDenied: Location access denied. Please use ZIP code instead.
weather.locationUnavailable: Location unavailable. Please enter a ZIP code.
weather.locationTimeout: Location request timed out. Please enter a ZIP code.
weather.zipInvalid: Please enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code.
weather.zipNotFound: ZIP code not found. Try a Southern California ZIP (90xxx–92xxx).
weather.uvWarning: UV index is {level}. Use sunscreen (SPF 30+), wear a hat, and seek shade during peak hours (10 AM – 4 PM).
weather.bestTidePoolingDesc: Best during low tides — check the tide chart for the lowest points.
weather.bestSurfingDesc: Best with consistent swells of 3+ ft and offshore winds.
weather.bestCleanupDesc: Best during low tides when more beach is exposed.
weather.bestWildlifeDesc: Best in early morning with calm conditions.
weather.marineUnavailable: Marine data unavailable for this location.
weather.noLocationMessage: Choose a beach or enter your ZIP code to see weather and tide conditions.

legal.privacyDescription: How we collect, use, and protect your personal information.
legal.privacyDraftNotice: This privacy policy is a draft and will be finalized before launch.
legal.privacyIntroText: Orca Child in the Wild ("OCINW", "we", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your personal information when you visit our website or participate in our programs.
legal.privacyCollectionText: We collect information you provide directly, such as your name, email address, and any details you share through our contact, volunteer, or donation forms. For minors under 13, we require verifiable parental consent before collecting any personal information (COPPA compliance).
legal.privacyCookiesText: We use essential cookies for website functionality (e.g., language preference, session management). We do not use third-party tracking cookies or advertising networks.
legal.privacyThirdPartyText: We use the following third-party services: Supabase (database hosting), Zeffy (donation processing), Resend (email delivery), and Vercel (website hosting). Each service has its own privacy policy governing data they process on our behalf.
legal.privacyCoppaText: We take children's privacy seriously. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 without verifiable parental consent. Parents can request deletion of their child's data at any time.
legal.privacyRetentionText: You may request deletion of your personal data at any time by contacting us. We use a soft-delete process and permanently remove data within 90 days of your request (CCPA compliance).
legal.privacyContactText: For privacy concerns, contact us at orcachildinthewild@gmail.com.
legal.termsDescription: Terms and conditions for using the Orca Child in the Wild website.
legal.termsDraftNotice: These terms of use are a draft and will be finalized before launch.
legal.termsIntroText: By accessing and using the Orca Child in the Wild website, you agree to these terms of use. If you do not agree, please do not use our website.
legal.termsUseText: This website is provided for informational, educational, and community engagement purposes. You may browse content, submit forms, and participate in our programs in accordance with these terms.
legal.termsContentText: Any content you submit through our forms (contact messages, volunteer applications, etc.) must be truthful, non-harmful, and comply with applicable laws. We reserve the right to remove content that violates these terms.
legal.termsDonationsText: Donations made through Zeffy are processed according to Zeffy's terms of service. Donation refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis — please contact us within 30 days if you need a refund.
legal.termsLiabilityText: OCINW provides this website 'as is' without warranties. We are not liable for any damages arising from your use of this website. Participation in conservation events is voluntary and at your own risk.
legal.termsGoverningText: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of California, United States.
legal.termsContactText: For questions about these terms, contact us at orcachildinthewild@gmail.com.
